be proud of what you’re enthusiastic about. stop pretending you’re indifferent! apathy is a repelling force! enthusiasm is magnetic! own your excitement, it’s meant to compel you to action and passionate speech.

very recently, i took an action that, in restrospect, my entire body and soul was telling me not to do.

"but i gotta, they are counting on me" was the internal narrative

so i did

and it blew up in my face. the action could not be undone, either, so now i eat the consequences

"procrastination gets a bad rap" can certainly be cope, but if we look at this outside of a black/white good/bad context... the opportunity is to tune in more deeply to myself and learn which body sensations mean "no, stop" and which mean "i'm just scared"

i am under no delusion here, either. i know that a lot of procrastination is just fear "dressing itself up as rational hesitation" but damn... so many times i have coerced myself into action only to find that i made a new problem that would have been avoided had i slowed down
i find that a good amount of my procrastination turns out to be intuitive protection
